Skip to contents
terra 1.9-22
  • Description
  • Tutorials
  • Reference
  • Changelog

Run app() or focal() over tiles using a future plan

future_app.Rd

Thin convenience wrappers around tile_apply that route the per-tile work through the future framework via future_lapply. They:

  • tile the input SpatRaster (auto-tiles by default),

  • ship x to the workers with wrap – file-backed sources travel as paths only; large in-memory rasters are materialised to a temporary file first, so very large rasters never have their values copied to every worker,

  • run app (future_app) or focal (future_focal) on each tile,

  • assemble the per-tile outputs back into a single SpatRaster.

The user controls parallelism by setting up a future::plan (e.g. future::multisession, future::cluster, future.batchtools, ...). With the default future::sequential plan the call still works, but runs in-process.

Usage

future_app(x, fun, ..., plan=NULL, cpkgs=NULL,
           tiles=NULL, buffer=0,
           filename="", overwrite=FALSE, wopt=list())

future_focal(x, w=3, fun="sum", ..., plan=NULL, cpkgs=NULL,
             tiles=NULL, buffer=NULL,
             filename="", overwrite=FALSE, wopt=list())

Arguments

x

SpatRaster

fun

a function as accepted by app (for future_app) or by focal (for future_focal). Forwarded verbatim to the per-tile call

w

focal window size or matrix; passed to focal

...

additional arguments forwarded to the per-tile app() / focal() call (e.g. na.rm=, fillvalue=, cores= for an inner per-tile parallelism, wopt=, ...)

plan

optional future plan or strategy object (e.g. future::multisession()). When NULL (the default), the currently-active future::plan() is used; the call does not modify it. When given, it is installed for the duration of the call and the previous plan is restored on exit

cpkgs

character. Names of additional packages that workers must load (e.g. "randomForest"). Passed to future.apply as future.packages = c("terra", cpkgs)

tiles

tile specification, see tile_apply. Defaults to NULL (auto tiles from GDAL block size + worker count)

buffer

integer read buffer (in cells) used when tiles = NULL, see tile_apply. For future_focal the default is floor(max(w)/2), which is wide enough that each per-tile focal() sees the same neighbours as a full-raster focal(); for future_app the default of 0 is correct because app() is per-cell

filename

output filename; empty (default) returns a VRT-backed SpatRaster, see tile_apply

overwrite

logical

wopt

list. Writing options as in writeRaster

Details

These are equivalent to:


tile_apply(x, function(z, ...) app(z, fun, ...),   ..., cores = "future")
tile_apply(x, function(z, ...) focal(z, w, fun, ...), ...,
           buffer = floor(max(w)/2), cores = "future")

with wrap()/unwrap() of x and any terra objects in ... done automatically. See tile_apply for the full machinery (auto-tiling, buffered tiles, VRT vs.\ mosaic assembly, and the disk-streaming guarantee).

The wrappers are intentionally minimal so they compose with the rest of the future ecosystem (handlers, plan stacks, remote workers, ...).

Value

A SpatRaster.

See also

tile_apply, app, focal, wrap, getTileExtents, future::plan, future.apply::future_lapply

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
if (requireNamespace("future", quietly=TRUE) &&
    requireNamespace("future.apply", quietly=TRUE)) {

    library(future)
    library(future.apply)
    plan(multisession, workers = 2)

    f <- system.file("ex/elev.tif", package="terra")
    r <- rast(f)

    # app(): per-cell. No buffer needed.
    out_a <- future_app(r, fun = function(v) v * 2)

    # focal(): auto-buffer = floor(w/2) keeps tile edges identical to a
    # full-raster focal().
    out_f <- future_focal(r, w = 5, fun = "mean", na.rm = TRUE)

    # explicit plan, restored on exit
    out_b <- future_app(r, fun = function(v) v + 1,
                        plan = sequential())

    plan(sequential)
}
} # }
